name: sf-wrap
description: Binary-assertion test suite for /sf:wrap. Each test provides a fixture session transcript + expected classifier label + expected wiki-page diff plan + expected feed-call args. Tests are run by /sf:improve-skill's Karpathy loop (per ADR-012) and by CI.
_status: scaffold — fixtures pending; assertions are the testable goals as /sf:wrap implementation lands. Schema conforms to ADR-011 §eval.json schema.

tests.expected_output_summary: Classifier returns 'none'; zero wiki edits except CONTEXT.md rewrite + log.md append; feed entry written.
binary_assertions: Classifier output 'labels' field equals exactly ['none']
No new file is created under wiki/projects/<active>/decisions/ after the run
No new file is created under wiki/projects/<active>/patterns/ after the run
wiki/projects/<active>/CONTEXT.md content differs from its pre-wrap state
wiki/projects/<active>/log.md has exactly one new line whose date matches today
Master wiki/log.md is byte-identical to its pre-wrap state (routine sessions skip master log)
feed.feed_write_session_end was called exactly once during the run
The feed_write_session_end call passed skip=False (no SF_SKIP_FEED env, no --skip-feed flag)

tests.expected_output_summary: Classifier returns 'decision'; new file under decisions/; STATE.md updated; master log appended.
binary_assertions: Classifier output 'labels' field contains 'decision'
A new file is created at wiki/projects/<active>/decisions/<slug>.md where <slug> is non-empty kebab-case
The new decisions file's YAML frontmatter includes 'framework_version', 'schema_version', and 'type: decision' fields
wiki/projects/<active>/STATE.md contains a new entry under the 'Recent decisions' section pointing at the new decisions file
Master wiki/log.md gains exactly one new entry whose date matches today (non-routine sessions update master log)

tests.expected_output_summary: First feed write rejected on a format-shape violation; one LLM re-prompt; second write succeeds; wiki updates remain intact.
binary_assertions: feed.feed_write_session_end is called exactly twice during the run
The first feed_write_session_end call returns FeedWriteResult(success=False) with a non-empty violation field
Between the two calls, the LLM is re-prompted with the violation reason from the first call
The second feed_write_session_end call returns FeedWriteResult(success=True)
All wiki diffs that the pre-feed step staged are applied (no rollback occurred)

tests.expected_output_summary: Skip-chain active; no feed write; wiki updates still applied; user sees 'feed skipped' message.
binary_assertions: feed.is_skip_active was called with wrap_flag=True and returned (True, 'wrap-flag')
feed.feed_write_session_end either was not invoked OR was invoked with skip=True and returned success=True with empty entry_id
The wiki diff plan was still applied (a decisions/<slug>.md file exists after the run)
The user-facing output contains the substring 'feed skipped' or '(feed skipped:'

tests.expected_output_summary: Mid-batch wiki write fails (simulated permission error); all wiki changes rolled back via git restore; feed call NOT made; user sees retry instructions.
binary_assertions: The command 'git restore wiki/' (or equivalent) was invoked during the run
After the run, wiki/ has zero diffs against its pre-run state (full rollback confirmed)
feed.feed_write_session_end was NOT invoked (consistency: wiki failed, feed deferred)
The user-facing output contains instructions explaining how to retry the wrap

non_triggers.prompt: Pin this for later: we decided to use Postgres.
What did we decide about auth last week?

_notes: Fixtures under fixtures/ are populated incrementally as /sf:wrap implementation lands.
Run via: uv run pytest skills/sf-wrap/eval/ (TBD wiring).
These assertions are also the targets for /sf:improve-skill's Karpathy loop per ADR-012.
